title: Baklava
ingredients: 1 cup honey (or slightly less, to taste)
instructions: In a bowl, mix walnuts, sugar, cinnamon, and cloves.
Butter the bottom of a large cookie sheet or jelly roll pan.
Layer 2 sheets of phyllo dough in the pan and brush with butter. Repeat until about ⅓ of the phyllo is used (approximately 6–8 sheets).
Sprinkle ½ of the nut mixture evenly over the phyllo and drizzle lightly with butter.
Add another third of the phyllo dough, buttering each layer.
Sprinkle remaining nut mixture over the phyllo.
Top with the remaining phyllo layers.
Cut the baklava into diamond shapes by first cutting vertical lines, then diagonal lines. Do not butter the top two sheets yet.
Bake until golden brown, about 35 minutes.
Remove from oven and let cool completely.
Meanwhile, combine lemon juice, honey, sugar, and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Pour hot syrup evenly over cooled baklava.
notes: A treasured recipe from my friend Lisa Ames.
